evals.prompt: Audit the web application security posture and produce a risk-sorted findings report
evals.expected_output: OWASP-aligned findings with severity, evidence, and remediation code for each issue

assertions.text: Output scans HTTP headers with curl or references securityheaders.com
Output checks for CSP header presence and strength
Output checks CORS configuration for wildcard or Origin reflection
Output checks cookie flags (SameSite, HttpOnly, Secure)
Output scans for dangerous patterns (dangerouslySetInnerHTML, eval)
Output runs npm audit or equivalent dependency check
Output provides CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW severity classification
Output provides code-level fixes with before/after examples
Output tags confirmed findings as [HECHO] with evidence
Output provides a prioritized remediation timeline

evals.prompt: Implement Content Security Policy for an Express.js app. Currently using inline scripts for Google Analytics and a React app
evals.expected_output: Nonce-based CSP implementation with report-only first, enforcement second, and GTM/Analytics handling

assertions.text: Output starts with Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only mode
Output generates nonce per request using crypto.randomBytes
Output uses nonce in CSP header: nonce-{value}
Output injects nonce into HTML templates/props
Output builds from default-src 'none'
Output includes frame-ancestors 'none' for clickjacking protection
Output includes base-uri 'self'
Output warns against unsafe-inline
Output recommends 2-week report-only phase before enforcement
Output provides csp-evaluator.withgoogle.com for validation

evals.prompt: The CORS implementation allows any origin with credentials. Fix it.
evals.expected_output: Allowlist-based CORS fix with Vary header, credentials handling, and tests to verify fix

assertions.text: Output finds the Origin reflection vulnerability in code
Output uses Set for allowlist lookup (not regex)
Output adds Vary: Origin header when CORS is conditional
Output explicitly blocks wildcard with credentials combination
Output handles localhost/development origin separately
Output provides curl test commands to verify fix
Output tests attacker origin is rejected
Output explains why Vary: Origin is required (CDN cache poisoning)

evals.prompt: Found dangerouslySetInnerHTML used with user display names throughout the React app. Fix XSS.
evals.expected_output: XSS remediation using DOMPurify or React auto-escaping, with Trusted Types for future prevention

assertions.text: Output scans all occurrences before fixing
Output determines if HTML rendering is actually needed for display names
Output recommends removing dangerouslySetInnerHTML if HTML is not needed
Output provides DOMPurify implementation where HTML is genuinely needed
Output specifies ALLOWED_TAGS allowlist in DOMPurify config
Output mentions Trusted Types as additional layer
Output mentions CSP as defense-in-depth layer
Output provides Playwright test to verify XSS payload is encoded

evals.prompt: Implement CSRF protection for a banking application with session cookies and 15 state-mutating API endpoints
evals.expected_output: SameSite cookies + CSRF token implementation with timing-safe comparison and frontend integration

assertions.text: Output implements SameSite=Strict on session cookie first
Output notes SameSite=Lax may be needed for OAuth flows
Output generates CSRF token using crypto.randomBytes
Output stores CSRF token in server session
Output validates CSRF token on all POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE routes
Output uses crypto.timingSafeEqual for token comparison
Output provides frontend code to include X-CSRF-Token header
Output provides test to verify cross-origin POST is rejected
Output mentions banking context requires SameSite=Strict (not Lax)
